title: Why USA is disadvantaged in AI Race
summary: A video analyzing why the USA faces infrastructure disadvantages in the AI race despite dominance in chips, models, and applications. The core gap is power generation and distribution: new AI data centers require 100MW to 2GW, and China's centralized grid planning through NDRC and NEA, combined with 25,000+ miles of UHV transmission lines, outpaces the fragmented US system across federal, state, and local jurisdictions. US hyperscalers like Google, Meta, OpenAI, and AWS are adapting through behind-the-meter deals and off-grid plants, with Texas and ERCOT emerging as preferred locations.
